name: node-ampproject-remapping
title: node-ampproject-remapping
summary: Nodejs module to remap sequential sourcemaps through transformations
description:
Nodejs module to remap sequential sourcemaps through transformations
Remapping allows one to take the sourcemaps generated through transforming
code and "remap" them to the original source locations. Think "minified code,
transformed with babel and bundled with webpack", all pointing to the correct
location in your original source code.
.
With remapping, none of source code transformations need to be aware of the
input's sourcemap, they only need to generate an output sourcemap. This
greatly simplifies building custom transformations.
use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use node-ampproject-remapping because Nodejs module to remap sequential sourcemaps through transformations.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use node-ampproject-remapping as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair node-ampproject-remapping with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `node-ampproject-remapping --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
